AD_FIELD.NAME: Payment Method
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: It is the method by which payment is expected to be made or received.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Starting Date
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A parameter stating the starting time range related to a specified request.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Date From indicates the starting date of a range.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Execute Payment
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Execute Payment
AD_FIELD.HELP: Execute Payment

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Ending Date
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A parameter stating the ending time range related for a specified request, query, etc.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Date To indicates the end date of a range (inclusive)

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Description
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Document Type
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A value defining what sequence and process setup are used to handle this document.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Document Type determines document sequence and processing rules

AD_FIELD.NAME: Document No.
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: An often automatically generated identifier for all documents.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The document number is usually automatically generated by the system and determined by the document type of the document. If the document is not saved, the preliminary number is displayed in "<>". If the document type of your document has no automatic document sequence defined, the field will be empty when creating a new document. This is for documents which usually have an external number (like vendor invoice). If you leave the field empty, the system will generate a document number for you. The document sequence used for this fallback number is defined in the "Document Sequence" window with the name "DocumentNo_<TableName>", where TableName is the actual name of the table inside the database (e.g. C_Order).

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Generated Credit
AD_FIELD.HELP: Amount left as credit on the payment.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Cost Center
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Division that adds to the cost of an organization
AD_FIELD.HELP: Division that adds to the cost of an organization. Internal structure for cost allocation.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Document No.
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: An often automatically generated identifier for all documents.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The document number is usually automatically generated by the system and determined by the document type of the document. If the document is not saved, the preliminary number is displayed in "<>". If the document type of your document has no automatic document sequence defined, the field will be empty when creating a new document. This is for documents which usually have an external number (like vendor invoice). If you leave the field empty, the system will generate a document number for you. The document sequence used for this fallback number is defined in the "Document Sequence" window with the name "DocumentNo_<TableName>", where TableName is the actual name of the table inside the database (e.g. C_Order).

AD_FIELD.NAME: Line No.
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A line stating the position of this request in the document.
AD_FIELD.HELP: Indicates the unique line for a document. It will also control the display order of the lines within a document.

AD_FIELD.NAME: G/L Item
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: An alias for the Account Combination which can be commonly used in daily operations.
AD_FIELD.HELP: General ledger item.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Invoice
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A document listing products, quantities and prices, payment terms, etc.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Invoice ID uniquely identifies an Invoice Document.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Financial Account
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Financial account used to deposit / withdrawal money such as bank accounts or petty cash
AD_FIELD.HELP: Financial account used to deposit / withdrawal money such as bank accounts or petty cash

AD_FIELD.NAME: Business Partner
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Anyone who takes part in daily business operations by acting as a customer, employee, etc.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Business Partner is anyone with whom you transact. This can include a customer, vendor, employee or any combination of these.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Line No.
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A line stating the position of this request in the document.
AD_FIELD.HELP: Indicates the unique line for a document. It will also control the display order of the lines within a document.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Write-off Amount
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A monetary sum that can be deducted from tax obligations.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Write Off Amount indicates the amount to be written off as uncollectible.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Organization
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_FIELD.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Organization
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_FIELD.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: sdcc_ChangeRPTStatus
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: sdcc_ChangeRPTStatus

AD_FIELD.NAME: Document No.
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: An often automatically generated identifier for all documents.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The document number is usually automatically generated by the system and determined by the document type of the document. If the document is not saved, the preliminary number is displayed in "<>". If the document type of your document has no automatic document sequence defined, the field will be empty when creating a new document. This is for documents which usually have an external number (like vendor invoice). If you leave the field empty, the system will generate a document number for you. The document sequence used for this fallback number is defined in the "Document Sequence" window with the name "DocumentNo_<TableName>", where TableName is the actual name of the table inside the database (e.g. C_Order).

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Organization
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_FIELD.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Business Partner
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Anyone who takes part in daily business operations by acting as a customer, employee, etc.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Business Partner is anyone with whom you transact. This can include a customer, vendor, employee or any combination of these.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Used Credit
AD_FIELD.HELP: If the payment has some generated credit is the amount already used on other payments. If the payment does not have a generated credit amount is the amount of credit used on the payment.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Reference No.
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The number for a specific reference.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The reference number can be printed on orders and invoices to allow your business partner to faster identify your records.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Total Paid
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Total amount paid.
AD_FIELD.HELP: Total amount paid.

AD_FIELD.NAME: 1st Dimension
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A display of optional elements that are previously defined for this account combination.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The user defined element displays the optional elements that have been defined for this account combination.
